sections.paragraph:
ἀπαθής, ές (s. πάσχω; Theognis I, 1177 et al.).

① pert. to not being subject to suffering, free of suffering (so Plato et al.; Stoic term: Stoic. II p. 99, 23; Teles p. 56, 14; Porphyr., Abst. 2, 61; Herm. Wr. 2, 12; Philo; Jos., Bell. 5, 417; POxy 526, 3f; Just., Ath. Cp. Theognis 1177; X., An., 7, 7, 33 al.) of Christ (opp. παθητός as in Proclus, Theol. 80 p. 74, 33) IEph 7:2; IPol 3:2.

② pert. to emergence from suffering or injury without mishap, without suffering, uninjured (Hdt. 1, 32; 2, 119; Dio Chrys. 19 [36], 40 Zoroaster emerges from fire unharmed; Appian, Liby. 111 §522; Jos., Ant. 1, 284; also med. term, e.g. Galen 5, 122) Βαρσαβᾶς … ἰὸν ἐχίδνης πιῶν … ἀ. διεφυλάχθη despite his having drunk snake-venom, he suffered no ill effects Papias (11:2).—TW.

Theognis Theognis , VI b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Stoic Stoic , various dates—List 5

Teles Teles , III b.c.—List 5

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5
  2. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

opp. opp. = opposed to, opposite

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

IPol IPol = Ignatius to Polycarp—List 1

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Dio Chrys Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

med. med. = medicus (medical writer), medical

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

Galen Galen , II a.d.—List 5

Papias Papias , early Christian writer, II a.d.—List 1

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 95
